user_symptom: Increased delays for CloudWatch log event processing for metric filter extraction and log subscriptions in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Increased error rates with the CloudWatch GetMetricStatistics and GetMetricData APIs when requesting metrics older than 3 hours
customers experienced intermittent delays in the availability of CloudWatch metrics for load balancers in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Users experienced delays in processing alarms for CloudWatch alarms APIs.

Some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch Metrics PutMetricData and CloudWatch Logs PutLogEvents APIs in the AP-EAST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state, and some metric data may be missing.
Increased error rates for CloudWatch Logs Insights queries and increased delays for log events in query results.
Between 7:01 AM and 4:20 PM PDT we experienced increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.

Some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch APIs in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

a small number of instances were unavailable due to power loss in a single Availability Zone (usw1-az1) in the US-WEST-1 Region. During this time, some customers also experienced insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks for instances that were not impacted by the power event.
Degraded performance for some EBS volumes in the US-EAST-2 Region. Some customers experienced insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks.
Increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms
Increased delays for CloudWatch log events, some delayed log events, and API faults
We are investigating elevated rate of API faults and delays in processing some alarms in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We can confirm that scheduled events are not being delivered via CloudWatch Events in the US-EAST-1 Region.
AWS Lambda functions that are invoked in response to CloudWatch events may not be getting invoked successfully.
Starting at 5:27 PM PDT, there were delays delivering scheduled CloudWatch events.

Some customers experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.
Users experienced elevated API fault rates and latencies in the EU-WEST-2 Region. Some metrics were delayed initially.
increased delays for CloudWatch ELB metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region

Increased API error rates and latencies in the US-WEST-1 Region. APIs also returned insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks and CloudWatch alarms transitioned into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state if set on the delayed metrics.
Increased connection errors and latencies for CloudWatch APIs, delayed CloudWatch metrics.
Increased delays for CloudWatch log event processing for metric filter extraction and log subscriptions
Increased error rates and delays for CloudWatch Logs Insights queries in the US-EAST-2 Region. Elevated error rates and delays when running CloudWatch Logs Insights queries in the US-EAST-2 Region.
customers experienced elevated errors listing metrics via the CloudWatch ListMetrics API and the CloudWatch Management Console

Users may experience delays in log events and metrics in the CloudWatch Logs APIs. Alarms may transition into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state due to delayed metrics. Customers will experience faults in the CloudWatch Logs Management Console.

Increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region.
increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms
Delays delivering scheduled CloudWatch events and errors in scheduled event creation, AWS Lambda functions not getting invoked successfully.

Some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch APIs in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

Some customers experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into the INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

Increased provisioning latencies for new load balancers, delayed propagation of load balancer CloudWatch metrics, inconsistent health check statuses from the DescribeTargetHealth API for Network Load Balancers.

Users experienced elevated faults when making GetMetricStatistics API requests for metrics data more than two days old in the US-EAST-1 Region. CloudWatch Dashboards were unavailable. CloudWatch logs customers experienced elevated API faults and delayed log events.

Some instances experienced connectivity issues within a single Availability Zone. Some EBS volumes within the affected Availability Zone are experiencing degraded performance. Launches of new EC2 instances and the creation of new EBS volumes experienced increased error rates.
